summary: Dan Shapiro shares a collection of Japanese-style table designs he's bookmarked as inspiration for a personal woodworking project. The post is a simple mood board of furniture from various specialty retailers and craftspeople. He notes his need to learn curve-cutting techniques for future builds. The post is a brief, casual sharing of design references rather than an analytical piece.
key_points: Curates Japanese table designs from multiple specialty furniture makers as inspiration for a personal project
Sources include Green Tea Designs, Haiku Designs, G. Kent Furniture, Ortiz Studios, and Contemporary Tansu
Acknowledges a skills gap in freehand curve-cutting that he needs to address
money_quotes: Need to brainstorm some possible upstairs tables… might as well post the results for everyone to see.
I have to figure out how to cut curves–I hate doing anything freehand, but I'll have to learn sooner or later.
key_insight: A brief glimpse into Shapiro's maker side, using his blog as a public notebook for collecting Japanese furniture design inspiration for a personal woodworking project.
